The error is still occurring in production.\n\n**Recommended Code Solution:**\n```python\ndef calculate_iv(chunk):\n    # Input validation before calculation\n    for idx, row in chunk.iterrows():\n        t = row['T']  # Time to expiration\n        F = row['F']  # Forward price\n        K = row['K']  # Strike price\n        \n        # Validation guards\n        if t <= 0:\n            logger.warning(f\"Invalid time parameter t={t} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}\")\n            continue\n        if F <= 0 or K <= 0:\n            logger.warning(f\"Invalid price parameters F={F}, K={K} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}\")\n            continue\n        \n        # Safe calculation with exception handling\n        try:\n            iv = py_vollib_vectorized.implied_volatility.vectorized_implied_volatility(\n                undiscounted_option_price, F, K, t, flag\n            )\n        except ZeroDivisionError:\n            logger.error(f\"Calculation failed for symbol {row.get('symbol')}: price={undiscounted_option_price}, F={F}, K={K}, t={t}\")\n            continue\n```\n\n#### 2. **Monitor Implementation (row['F'] > 0, f\"Invalid F={row['F']}\"),\n            (row['K'] > 0, f\"Invalid K={row['K']}\"),\n            (row['undiscounted_option_price'] > 0, f\"Invalid  def __init__(self, failure_threshold=10, timeout=300):\n        self.failure_threshold = failure_threshold\n        self.timeout = timeout\n        self.failures = 0\n        self.last_failure_time = None\n        self.state = 'CLOSED'\n    \n    def call(self, func, *args, **kwargs):\n        if self.state == 'OPEN':\n            if time.time() - self.last_failure_time > self.timeout:\n                self.state = 'HALF_OPEN'\n            else:\n                raise CircuitBreakerOpenError(\"Circuit breaker is OPEN\")\n        \n        try:\n            result = func(*args, **kwargs)\n            if self.state == 'HALF_OPEN':\n                self.state = 'CLOSED'\n                self.failures = 0\n            return result\n        except ZeroDivisionError:\n            self.failures += 1\n            self.last_failure_time = time.time()\n            if self.failures >= self.failure_threshold:\n                self.state = 'OPEN'\n            raise\n```\n\n---\n\n## Applications on ardenone-cluster live on\n4\t# ardenone-manager's ArgoCD, which exposes a no-auth read-only proxy the fetch\n5\t# strand can consume directly. apexalgo-iad and the iad-* Spot clusters are\n6\t# managed by the ArgoCD instance on rs-manager, which today has NO equivalent\n7\t# no-auth read-only proxy — only the authenticated, Tailscale-only API.\n8\t#\n9\t# Querying the wrong instance returns not-found (indistinguishable from \"app\n10\t# doesn't exist\"). The fetch strand therefore resolves the ArgoCD endpoint from\n11\t# a registry entry's `cluster` through THIS file — and any cluster absent from\n12\t# here, or mapped to an `access` mode the fetch strand cannot satisfy (it holds\n13\t# no ArgoCD credentials, so only `read-only-proxy` is consumable), resolves the\n14\t# ArgoCD source as FAILED with a fetch_coverage caveat. Never a silent\n15\t# wrong-instance query.\n16\t#\n17\t# See docs/plan/plan.md → Fetch Strand → Cluster→ArgoCD Endpoint Resolution.\n18\t# Hot-reloaded via an mtime-checked cache (src/fetch/clusters.py) like every\n19\t# config artifact — edit takes effect on the next fetch, no restart.\n20\t#\n21\t# `access` modes:\n22\t#   read-only-proxy  → no auth; a proxy injects a read-only token. CONSUMABLE.\n23\t#   authenticated    → requires credentials the fetch strand does not hold.\n24\t#                      Resolves as failed with an honest caveat.\n25\tclusters:\n26\t  # --- ardenone-manager ArgoCD (no-auth read-only proxy) ---\n27\t  ardenone-cluster:\n28\t    argocd_api: https://argocd-ro-ardenone-manager-ts.ardenone.com:8444\n29\t    access: read-only-proxy   # ArgoCD on ardenone-manager; proxy injects a RO token\n30\t  ardenone-manager:\n31\t    argocd_api: https://argocd-ro-ardenone-manager-ts.ardenone.com:8444\n32\t    access: read-only-proxy   # ArgoCD runs on ardenone-manager itself\n33\t\n34\t  # --- rs-manager ArgoCD (authenticated only — no no-auth RO proxy today) ---\n35\t  apexalgo-iad:\n36\t    argocd_api: https://argocd-rs-manager.tail1b1987.ts.net:8080\n37\t    access: authenticated      # ⚠ no no-auth RO proxy for rs-manager — known issue\n38\t  iad-options:\n39\t    argocd_api: https://argocd-rs-manager.tail1b1987.ts.net:8080\n40\t    access: authenticated\n41\t  iad-kalshi:\n42\t    argocd_api: https://argocd-rs-manager.tail1b1987.ts.net:8080\n43\t    access: authenticated\n44\t  iad-ci:\n45\t    argocd_api: https://argocd-rs-manager.tail1b1987.ts.net:8080\n46\t    access: authenticated\n47\t  iad-native-ads:\n48\t    argocd_api: https://argocd-rs-manager.tail1b1987.ts.net:8080\n49\t    access: authenticated\n50\t  rs-manager:\n51\t    argocd_api: https://argocd-rs-manager.tail1b1987.ts.net:8080\n52\t    access: authenticated\n53\t\n54\t  # Clusters intentionally NOT mapped here (e.g. ardenone-hub, ord-devimprint)\n55\t  # resolve the ArgoCD source as failed with a caveat — preferred over a guess.\n56\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_2b75e5fb59e74b5bae746780","session_id":"87b6af13-efc5-4990-8955-ec926bd3f45d","uuid":"14393cda-32d5-4a94-ab64-ffe32e60dd6d","timestamp":"2026-07-24T13:08:09.567Z","subagent_type":"general-purpose","task_description":"Find Options Pipeline logs"}

FetchConfigValidationError(Exception):\n79\t    \"\"\"Raised when the fetch config schema is invalid.\"\"\"\n80\t    def __init__(self, errors: list[str]):\n81\t        self.errors = errors\n82\t        message = \"Fetch config validation failed:\\n\" + \"\\n\".join(f\"  - {e}\" for e in errors)\n83\t        super().__init__(message)\n84\t\n85\t\n86\tdef _validate_timeout_ms(value: Any, source_name: str) -> int | None:\n87\t    \"\"\"\n88\t    Validate a timeout_ms value.\n89\t\n90\t    Args:\n91\t        value: The timeout value to validate\n92\t        source_name: Name of the timeout value is invalid\n99\t    \"\"\"\n100\t    if value is None:\n101\t        return None\n102\t\n103\t    # Must be an integer or float that can be converted to int\n104\t    if not isinstance(value, (int, float)):\n105\t        raise FetchConfigValidationError([\n106\t            f\"{source_name}: timeout_ms must be a number, got {type(value).__name__}\"\n107\t        ])\n108\t\n109\t    # Convert to seconds (timeout_ms is in milliseconds)\n110\t    timeout_seconds = int(value) / 1000\n111\t\n112\t    # Must be positive\n113\t    if timeout_seconds <= 0:\n114\t _load_fetch_config() -> dict:\n122\t    \"\"\"\n123\t    Load fetch configuration from YAML file.\n124\t\n125\t    Returns:\n126\t        Dictionary with source timeouts. Empty dict if file not found or invalid.\n127\t\n128\t    Raises:\n129\t        FetchConfigValidationError: If config schema is invalid\n130\t    \"\"\"\n131\t    global _fetch_config_cache, _fetch_config_mtime\n132\t\n133\t    # File doesn't exist or can't be accessed\n140\t        return {}\n141\t\n142\t    # Load the YAML file\n143\t    try:\n144\t        config = yaml.safe_load(FETCH_CONFIG_PATH.read_text()) or {}\n145\t    except OSError:\n146\t        return {}  # File doesn't exist\n147\t\n148\t    # Validate the config structure\n149\t    errors = []\n150\t\n151\t    if \"sources\" in config:\n152\t        if not isinstance(config[\"sources\"], dict):\n153\t            errors.append(\"'sources' must be a dictionary\")\n154\t        else:\n155\t            # Project-specific override (if project_slug provided)\n184\t    2. Global source-specific timeout\n185\t    3. None (use default from spec)\n186\t\n187\t    Args:\n188\t        source: The fetch source to get timeout for\n189\t        project_slug: Optional project slug for project-specific overrides\n190\t\n191\t    Returns:\n192\t        Timeout in seconds, or None if not configured\n193\t\n194\t    Raises:\n195\t        FetchConfigValidationError: If config is invalid (on hot-reload)\n196\t    \"\"\"\n197\t    try:\n198\t        config = _load_fetch_config()\n199\t    except FetchConfigValidationError:\n200\t        # **Fix ZeroDivisionError in Options Pipeline**\n\n**Priority:** CRITICAL - Active production issue  \n**Impact:** Eliminates primary failure mode  \n\n**Recommended Code Solution:**\n```python\ndef calculate_iv(chunk):\n    \"\"\"Calculate implied volatility with proper input validation\"\"\"\n    for idx, row in chunk.iterrows():\n        t = row['T']  # Time to expiration\n        F = row['F']  # Forward price  \n        K = row['K']  # Strike price\n        price = row['undiscounted_option_price']\n        \n        # Pre-calculation validation\n        if t <= 0:\n            logger.warning(f\"Invalid T={t} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}, skipping\")\n            continue\n        if F <= 0 or K <= 0:\n            logger.warning(f\"Invalid price parameters F={F}, K={K} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}, skipping\")\n            continue\n        if price <= 0:\n            logger.warning(f\"Invalid option price={price} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}, skipping\")\n            continue\n        \n        # Safe calculation with exception handling\n        try:\n            iv = py_vollib_vectorized.implied_volatility.vectorized_implied_volatility(\n                price, F, K, t, flag\n            )\n        except (ZeroDivisionError, ValueError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Calculation failed for symbol {row.get('symbol')}: {e}\")\n            continue\n        \n        chunk.at[idx, 'IV'] = iv\n    return chunk\n```\n\n**Deployment Steps:**\n1. Update calculation code in options pipeline\n2. Add comprehensive input validation\n3. Implement graceful error handling\n4. Add monitoring for validation failures\n5. Deploy to canary environment first\n6. Monitor for ZeroDivisionError elimination\n\n#### 2. **Monitor Implementation Effectiveness**\n\n**After implementing the fix:**\n```bash\n# Monitor for ZeroDivisionError elimination  \nkubectl self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n    \n    def validate_row(self, row):\n        \"\"\"Validate a single options data row\"\"\"\n        checks = [\n            (row['T'] > 0, f\"Invalid T={row['T']}\"),\n            (row['F'] > 0, f\"Invalid F={row['F']}\"),\n            (row['K'] > 0, f\"Invalid K={row['K']}\"),\n            (row['undiscounted_option_price'] > 0, f\"Invalid price={row['undiscounted_option_price']}\")\n        ]\n        \n        for valid, error_msg in checks:\n            if not valid:\n                self.logger.warning(f\"Data validation failed: {error_msg} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}\")\n                return False\n        return True\n    \n    def filter_chunk(self, chunk):\n        \"\"\"Filter out invalid rows from a chunk\"\"\"\n        valid_rows = []\n        for idx, row in chunk.iterrows():\n            if self.validate_row(row):\n                valid_rows.append(row)\n        return valid_rows\n```\n\n**Integration Point:** Pre-calculation in the processing pipeline\n\n#### 4. **Add Telemetry for Data Quality**\n\n**Prometheus Metrics:**\n```python\nfrom prometheus_client import Counter, after threshold\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, failure_threshold=10, timeout=300):\n        self.failure_threshold = failure_threshold\n        self.timeout = timeout\n        self.failures = 0\n        self.last_failure_time = None\n        self.state = 'CLOSED'  # CLOSED, OPEN, HALF_OPEN\n    \n    def call(self, func, *args, **kwargs):\n        \"\"\"Execute function with circuit breaker protection\"\"\"\n        if self.state == 'OPEN':\n            if time.time() - self.last_failure_time > self.timeout:\n                self.state = 'HALF_OPEN'\n            else:\n                raise CircuitBreakerOpenError(\"Circuit breaker is OPEN - too many recent failures\")\n        \n        try:\n            result = func(*args, **kwargs)\n            if self.state == 'HALF_OPEN':\n                self.state = 'CLOSED'\n                self.failures = 0\n            return result\n        except ZeroDivisionError:\n            self.failures += 1\n            self.last_failure_time = time.time()\n            if self.failures >= self.failure_threshold:\n                self.state = 'OPEN'\n            raise\n```\n\n**Configuration:** 10 failures → 5-minute cooldown period\n\n#### **Fix ZeroDivisionError in Options Pipeline**\n\n**Priority:** CRITICAL - Active production issue  \n**Impact:** Eliminates primary failure mode  \n\n**Recommended Code Solution:**\n```python\ndef calculate_iv(chunk):\n    \"\"\"Calculate implied volatility with proper input validation\"\"\"\n    for idx, row in chunk.iterrows():\n        t = row['T']  # Time to expiration\n        F = row['F']  # Forward price  \n        K = row['K']  # Strike price\n        price = row['undiscounted_option_price']\n        \n        # Pre-calculation validation\n        if t <= 0:\n            logger.warning(f\"Invalid T={t} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}, skipping\")\n            continue\n        if F <= 0 or K <= 0:\n            logger.warning(f\"Invalid price parameters F={F}, K={K} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}, skipping\")\n            continue\n        if price <= 0:\n            logger.warning(f\"Invalid option price={price} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}, skipping\")\n            continue\n        \n        # Safe calculation with exception handling\n        try:\n            iv = py_vollib_vectorized.implied_volatility.vectorized_implied_volatility(\n                price, F, K, t, flag\n            )\n        except (ZeroDivisionError, ValueError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Calculation failed for symbol {row.get('symbol')}: {e}\")\n            continue\n        \n        chunk.at[idx, 'IV'] = iv\n    return chunk\n```\n\n**Deployment Steps:**\n1. Update calculation code in options pipeline\n2. Add comprehensive input validation\n3. Implement graceful error handling\n4. Add monitoring for validation failures\n5. Deploy to canary environment first\n6. self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n    \n    def validate_row(self, row):\n        \"\"\"Validate a single options data row\"\"\"\n        checks = [\n            (row['T'] > 0, f\"Invalid T={row['T']}\"),\n            (row['F'] > 0, f\"Invalid F={row['F']}\"),\n            (row['K'] > 0, f\"Invalid K={row['K']}\"),\n            (row['undiscounted_option_price'] > 0, f\"Invalid price={row['undiscounted_option_price']}\")\n        ]\n        \n        for valid, error_msg in checks:\n            if not valid:\n                self.logger.warning(f\"Data validation failed: {error_msg} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}\")\n                return False\n        return True\n    \n    def filter_chunk(self, chunk):\n        \"\"\"Filter out invalid rows from a chunk\"\"\"\n        valid_rows = []\n        for idx, row in chunk.iterrows():\n            if self.validate_row(row):\n                valid_rows.append(row)\n        return valid_rows\n```\n\n**Integration Point:** Pre-calculation in the processing pipeline\n\n#### 4. **Add Telemetry for Data Quality**\n\n**Prometheus Metrics:**\n```python\nfrom prometheus_client import Counter,        self.last_failure_time = None\n        self.state = 'CLOSED'  # CLOSED, OPEN, HALF_OPEN\n    \n    def call(self, func, *args, **kwargs):\n        \"\"\"Execute function with circuit breaker protection\"\"\"\n        if self.state == 'OPEN':\n            if time.time() - self.last_failure_time > self.timeout:\n                self.state = 'HALF_OPEN'\n            else:\n                raise CircuitBreakerOpenError(\"Circuit breaker is OPEN - too many recent failures\")\n        \n        try:\n            result = func(*args, **kwargs)\n            if self.state == 'HALF_OPEN':\n                self.state = 'CLOSED'\n                self.failures = 0\n            return result\n        except ZeroDivisionError:\n            self.failures += 1\n            self.last_failure_time = time.time()\n            if self.failures >= self.failure_threshold:\n                self.state = 'OPEN'\n            raise\n```\n\n**Configuration:** 10 failures → 5-minute cooldown period\n\n####
